title: Downed with scars
text: Defeat drops you hard but not out of the story; recovery leaves marks that matter.

title: Rare true death
text: True death is possible on brutal choices; most failures are costly survivals.

title: Faction ransom death
text: Death often becomes capture; factions buy, trade, or bury you for leverage.

title: Honor funeral stakes
text: How you die changes reputation tracks more than loot tables.

title: Soft setback, hard cost
text: Narrative setbacks replace delete; time, gear, and pride are the reload fee.

title: Practical scarce finds
text: Boots, rope, permits, medicine; epic sparkles are rumors until late.

title: Information as loot
text: Maps, passwords, seals, and names beat gold in intrigue worlds.

title: Corpo scrip and licenses
text: Company money and licenses beat cash; black market converts at cruel rates.

title: Sect technique token
text: Paper tokens that unlock training hours; forged tokens are death sentences.

title: Quest-gated materials
text: Upgrade materials drop from problems solved, not random sparkle piles.

title: Cursed but useful
text: Best early gear has a social or physical tax; free power is bait.

title: Ration chit currency
text: Food rights as money; forged chits start wars at the queue.

title: Named specialists rare
text: Most people are ordinary; specialists have names, rates, and schedules.

title: Relative power ladder
text: NPC threat scales relative to player rank and local context, not global god stats.

title: Faction face characters
text: Each faction has a public face, a quiet hand, and a disposable enforcer.
examples: smile is the face, knife is the hand

title: Mentors cost something
text: Teachers want money, loyalty, risk, or humiliation, never free montages.

title: Rival as mirror
text: A rival shares your domain half a step ahead or behind; growth is comparative.

title: Crowd as pressure
text: Crowds witness, record, and punish face loss; empty rooms are different games.

title: Child witness complication
text: A child saw too much; protecting them is a quest with social explosives.

title: Job board first coin
text: Start at a board of bad jobs; the least bad one still has teeth.
examples: missing cat is not a cat

title: Debt notice on the door
text: A notice names a sum and a deadline; story begins as accounting.

title: Wrong body, right skills
text: Wake in a body with muscle memory that is not yours and papers that almost fit.

title: Train arrives early
text: A train dumps you in a town that expected someone else with your ticket.

title: Ritual interrupted mid-chant
text: You are the interruption; the circle still smokes and someone blames you.

title: System pings a local job
text: A window assigns a petty job with outsized consequences if ignored.

title: Isekai fair edge
text: Other world, no free OP, compounding seed skill, injuries and scarcity keep teeth.

title: Urban fantasy undercity
text: Modern streets over older laws; magic hides in service corridors and late trains.

title: Street-level cape work
text: Powers exist but bills do too; cape work is a night job with PR problems.

title: Post-magic wasteland
text: Magic broke the world already; survivors treat spells like radiation.

title: Steampunk fog empire
text: Brass, smog, airships, and class war under clocktowers.

title: Hopeful grit
text: Hard world, stubborn kindness; victories are small and kept.

title: Satirical bite
text: Systems and pomp get mocked; people still bleed when jokes land wrong.

title: Epic distant thunder
text: Big history moves offscreen; your scenes stay local until you force scale.
examples: war is a rumor with smoke

title: Intimate close camera
text: Focus on faces, hands, and small rooms; the world peeks in, does not swallow scenes.

title: Ration harbor after desk death
text: Transmigration into dock labor and debt; former desk life is a liability, not a superpower.

title: One-skill compound isekai
text: Exactly one weak seed domain compounds through risk and training; no free combat kit.
